name: Apricot (or Peach) Honey Jam
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the apricots and cut them in half through the natural indentation.
Remove the pits and slice each half into 4 lengthwise strips.
There should be approximately 4 quarts of fruit.
2. Gently combine the apricot slices, sugar, honey, and lemon juice in a large
nonreactive bowl. Allow to stand at room temperature for 3 to 4 hours,
stirring several times to keep the fruit coated and to help the juices
dissolve the sugar.
3. Pour the fruit mixture into a large 6-quart nonreactive shallow preserving pan.
Bring to a boil over high heat, skimming off any foam with a metal spoon or fine mesh skimmer.
4. Reduce heat to medium and continue cooking, stirring occasionally, for 20 to 30 minutes
until the mixture is thick with a few chunks left and appears slightly glazed.
5. Turn off the heat and skim any additional foam. Ladle into hot sterilized jars,
wipe rims clean with a damp towel, and seal with new lids and metal rings.
6. Process in a hot-water bath for 5 minutes. Remove, let cool, check seals,
label, and store.
Makes 9 cups. Per tablespoon: 23 calories; trace fat
